**Coverage notes:** All 1,198 official paper rows were screened and seven eligible papers were cataloged. The accepted Resource roster, special and Health paper mappings, and the PromptEng and WebST workshop programs remain unresolved. Workshop is omitted from tracks\_checked despite one cataloged workshop record because the workshop universe remains incomplete.

7 verified papers curated for direct relevance to quantitative finance and asset management. Tracks are separated so main-conference, position, and workshop status remains visible.

## Main Conference (6)

### [Analysis of CEX-DEX Arbitrage Opportunities with Hidden Markov Models](<https://doi.org/10.1145/3774904.3792185>)

**Catalog ID:** 2026-www-ladoczki-cex-dex-arbitrage

**Authors:** Bence Ladóczki

**Venue / year:** WWW · 2026

**Track / presentation:** Main · Not Specified

**Subvenue:** Economics, Online Markets and Human Computation

**Status / verified:** Accepted · 2026-07-12

**Topics:** Market Microstructure · Evaluation

**Assets / frequency:** Crypto

**Tasks:** CEX-DEX arbitrage opportunity analysis · arbitrage trade and profit estimation

**Identifiers:** DOI 10.1145/3774904.3792185

**Focus:** Uses hidden Markov models to characterize CEX-DEX price gaps and estimate arbitrage-trade counts and profits in Ethereum markets.

**Why it matters:** Measures cross-venue crypto arbitrage opportunities and their economic value.

**Links:** [Official venue record](<https://github.com/thewebconf2026/www2026/blob/beba571d4f3056bb2e137d245802b3c3ebf235eb/accepted/Accepted%20papers%20-%20Main%20proceedings%20-%20Main%20Research%20track%20-%20rfp.csv#L10>) · [Paper](<https://doi.org/10.1145/3774904.3792185>)

### [Financial Wind Tunnel: A Retrieval-Augmented Market Simulator](<https://arxiv.org/abs/2503.17909>)

**Catalog ID:** 2026-www-cao-financial-wind-tunnel

**Authors:** Bokai Cao, Xueyuan Lin, Yiyan Qi, Chengjin Xu, Cehao Yang, Jian Guo

**Venue / year:** WWW · 2026

**Track / presentation:** Main · Not Specified

**Subvenue:** Industry

**Status / verified:** Accepted · 2026-07-12

**Topics:** Market Simulation · Synthetic Data · Risk Management · Evaluation

**Assets / frequency:** Mixed

**Tasks:** market scenario generation · quantitative-model stress testing · risk-aware downstream optimization

**Identifiers:** arXiv 2503.17909

**Focus:** Generates controllable cross-frequency market scenarios and uses them to stress-test and optimize downstream quantitative models under return and risk objectives.

**Why it matters:** Provides decision-relevant synthetic scenarios for robustness testing and risk-aware model selection.

**Links:** [Official venue record](<https://github.com/thewebconf2026/www2026/blob/beba571d4f3056bb2e137d245802b3c3ebf235eb/accepted/Accepted%20papers%20-%20Main%20proceedings%20-%20Industry.csv#L38>) · [Paper](<https://arxiv.org/abs/2503.17909>)

### [LiquidityPool: Game-Theoretic Analysis of Stakeholder Revenue in Ranking-Dependent DeFi](<https://doi.org/10.1145/3774904.3792260>)

**Catalog ID:** 2026-www-chen-liquiditypool

**Authors:** Qinde Chen, Huawei Huang, Jian Zheng

**Venue / year:** WWW · 2026

**Track / presentation:** Main · Not Specified

**Subvenue:** Economics, Online Markets and Human Computation

**Status / verified:** Accepted · 2026-07-12

**Topics:** Market Microstructure

**Assets / frequency:** Crypto

**Tasks:** DeFi participation mechanism analysis · stakeholder revenue analysis

**Identifiers:** DOI 10.1145/3774904.3792260

**Focus:** Analyzes how a ranking-dependent DeFi pooling mechanism changes participant revenue, concentration, welfare, and access on Ethereum.

**Why it matters:** Provides a market-structure analysis of fund participation and revenue distribution in a crypto protocol.

**Links:** [Official venue record](<https://github.com/thewebconf2026/www2026/blob/beba571d4f3056bb2e137d245802b3c3ebf235eb/accepted/Accepted%20papers%20-%20Main%20proceedings%20-%20Main%20Research%20track%20-%20rfp.csv#L16>) · [Paper](<https://doi.org/10.1145/3774904.3792260>)

### [Resisting Manipulative Bots in Meme Coin Copy Trading: A Multi-Agent Approach with Chain-of-Thought Reasoning](<https://arxiv.org/abs/2601.08641>)

**Catalog ID:** 2026-www-luo-meme-coin-copy-trading

**Authors:** Yichen Luo, Yebo Feng, Jiahua Xu, Yang Liu

**Venue / year:** WWW · 2026

**Track / presentation:** Main · Not Specified

**Subvenue:** Security and Privacy

**Status / verified:** Accepted · 2026-07-12

**Topics:** Financial Agents · Risk Management · Evaluation

**Assets / frequency:** Crypto

**Tasks:** copy-trading decision defense · manipulative-bot robustness · realized-return evaluation

**Identifiers:** arXiv 2601.08641 · DOI 10.1145/3774904.3792635

**Focus:** Develops a multi-agent defense for meme-coin copy trading and evaluates trading returns under manipulative bots and market frictions.

**Why it matters:** Tests whether an automated trading decision can remain economically robust to adversarial manipulation.

**Links:** [Official venue record](<https://github.com/thewebconf2026/www2026/blob/beba571d4f3056bb2e137d245802b3c3ebf235eb/accepted/Accepted%20papers%20-%20Main%20proceedings%20-%20Main%20Research%20track%20-%20rfp.csv#L523>) · [Paper](<https://arxiv.org/abs/2601.08641>)

### [When Agents Trade: Live Multi-Market Trading Arena for LLM Agents](<https://arxiv.org/abs/2510.11695>)

**Catalog ID:** 2026-www-qian-when-agents-trade

**Authors:** Lingfei Qian, Xueqing Peng, Hanley Smith, Yi Han, Yueru He, Haohang Li, Yupeng Cao, Yangyang Yu, Guojun Xiong, Peng Lu, Yan Wang, Vincent Jim Zhang, Huan He, Alejandro Lopez-Lira, Jimin Huang, Jian-Yun Nie, Sophia Ananiadou

**Venue / year:** WWW · 2026

**Track / presentation:** Main · Not Specified

**Subvenue:** Industry

**Status / verified:** Accepted · 2026-07-12

**Topics:** Financial Agents · Risk Management · Evaluation

**Assets / frequency:** Equities, Crypto

**Tasks:** live multi-market trading · LLM trading-agent evaluation

**Identifiers:** arXiv 2510.11695

**Focus:** Builds a live arena that continuously evaluates LLM agents making stock and cryptocurrency trades under distinct risk styles.

**Why it matters:** Moves financial-agent evaluation from static answers to realized multi-market trading decisions and outcomes.

**Notes:** The official accepted title and author order differ from the later arXiv title and order; this record preserves the conference CSV metadata.

**Links:** [Official venue record](<https://github.com/thewebconf2026/www2026/blob/beba571d4f3056bb2e137d245802b3c3ebf235eb/accepted/Accepted%20papers%20-%20Main%20proceedings%20-%20Industry.csv#L15>) · [Paper](<https://arxiv.org/abs/2510.11695>)
